prerequisites: A Wrike account with access to the data you want to sync. The permanent access token inherits all of your Wrike permissions, so use an account whose access matches the projects, folders, and tasks you intend to extract.
A permanent access token generated from the Wrike workspace under the profile menu > Apps & Integrations > API (used for the required access_token field).

The Wrike data center host for your account (for the required wrike_instance field). Wrike stores customer data in several data centers in the USA and the European Union, and you must target the base URL that matches where your account's data lives.

authMethods.name: Permanent access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Wrike permanent access token supplied in the access_token field. The token is sent in the Authorization: Bearer header on every API v4 request and inherits all of the generating user's Wrike permissions. Treat it like a password and revoke it in Wrike if it is compromised.

setupSteps.title: Generate a permanent access token in Wrike
setupSteps.body: In your Wrike workspace, click your profile icon in the navigation bar and select Apps & Integrations, then click API. Click + App, enter an integration name, then click Get Token. Copy and store the token immediately, because it is not shown again after you close the page. The token inherits all of your Wrike permissions.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Wrike data center host
setupSteps.body: Determine the base host for your account's data center. Wrike serves accounts from multiple data centers in the USA and the European Union, and API requests must go to the host where your account's data is stored (the API base URL is https://<host>/api/v4). Using the wrong host returns a 401 error even with a valid token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the permanent access token in access_token (required) and your data center host in wrike_instance (required). Optionally set start_date to bound how far back the connector reads. Run the connector to verify that authentication succeeds and data is returned.

sources.title: Wrike: Legacy Authentication - Permanent Access Token
Wrike: OAuth 2.0 Authorization (data center host parameter)
